text: {{c1::Serving SLA}} defines the acceptable latency, throughput, and availability targets for an ML system in production.
explanation: It anchors design choices around user expectations and business constraints.
usage: Essential in high-scale recommendation, search, and fraud systems.
spanish: El serving SLA define los targets aceptables de latencia, throughput y disponibilidad para un sistema ML en producción.

text: A {{c1::model registry}} stores versions, metadata, and lineage so teams can track and deploy models safely.
explanation: It is a foundation of reliable MLOps and governance workflows.
usage: Used in enterprise ML platforms and regulated environments.
spanish: Un model registry almacena versiones, metadata y lineage para que los equipos puedan rastrear y desplegar modelos de forma segura.

text: {{c1::Canary deployments}} release a model to a small share of traffic before full rollout.
explanation: They reduce the blast radius of bad releases and make rollout safer.
usage: Common for ranking, fraud, and personalization models.
spanish: Los canary deployments lanzan un modelo a una pequeña fracción del tráfico antes del rollout completo.

text: Feature computation {{c1::pipelines}} transform raw events into consistent features for both training and online serving.
explanation: They are a core component of scalable and reliable ML platforms.
usage: Used in credit scoring, ads, recommendations, and fraud detection.
spanish: Las pipelines de computación de features transforman eventos crudos en features consistentes para entrenamiento y serving online.

text: {{c1::Monitoring}} and alerting detect prediction drift, data quality issues, and sudden performance degradation.
explanation: They are as important as model training for maintaining trust in production systems.
usage: Essential in production analytics and enterprise ML operations.
spanish: El monitoring y alerting detectan drift de predicción, problemas de calidad de datos y degradación repentina de rendimiento.

text: {{c1::Backpressure}} and autoscaling manage load spikes by controlling request rates and scaling infrastructure dynamically.
explanation: They are essential for maintaining stable serving under traffic bursts.
usage: Critical in web-scale AI APIs and high-traffic assistant products.
spanish: El backpressure y autoscaling gestionan picos de carga controlando tasas de solicitud y escalando infraestructura dinámicamente.
